channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Life sentence for driver in deadly car chase fuelled by jealous rage
item.description: A man is sentenced to life behind bars for the murder of 22-year-old Dale Aaron McKenna in Queensland's Western Downs.
item.dc:creator: Cindy Wockner and Grace Nakamura

media:group.media:description: Clinton Gordon Sutcliffe, pictured, was found guilty of the murder of Dale Aaron McKenna.

item.title: Judge's social media posts at centre of sexual abuse verdict appeal
item.description: A former Hobart teacher sued by a former student over allegations of repeated sexual abuse has filed an appeal on the grounds of social media posts made by the judge presiding over his case.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Jaggers

media:group.media:description: Stephen Fane Noga, who was sued last month over allegations of repeated sexual abuse, has filed an appeal in the Hobart Supreme Court.

item.title: Cage fighter guilty of manslaughter after sparring session death of friend
item.description: Bradley Fletcher has been found not guilty of murder but guilty of manslaughter for killing his best friend during a backyard sparring session and leaving his body by a footpath.
item.dc:creator: Jamie McKinnell

media:group.media:description: Bradley Fletcher has been found guilty of manslaughter for killing Bradley Evennett after a backyard sparring session in March 2024.

item.title: Breaking: Record payout after billionaire built mine without traditional owners' permission
item.description: Andrew Forrest's Fortescue has been ordered to pay $150 million to WA traditional owners after mining their land without permission.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Mayes, Mietta Adams, and Alistair Bates

media:group.media:description: A composite image of Andrew Forrest (left) and Michael Woodley situated at a Fortescue mine in WA.

item.title: Qld government to continue with Paradise Dam rebuild despite advice
item.description: The board of Sunwater has written to the state government, labelling the rebuild of Paradise Dam "grossly disproportionate" and should be "no further considered".
item.dc:creator: Claudia Williams

media:group.media:description: The government says it will push on with the Paradise Dam rebuild, despite advice against the project from Sunwater.

item.title: Government accused of 'burying' response to landmark gambling report
item.description: A long-awaited response to an inquiry that called for a gambling advertising ban has been tabled on budget day, prompting accusations the government is trying to bury the document.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Asher

media:group.media:description: A parliamentary report by late Labor MP Peta Murphy on gambling reform called for a total ban on gambling advertising.

item.title: Tax reform tipped to open door to 75,000 first home buyers
item.description: In a political push to get ahead of criticism it has broken election promises, Labor is making a case for how wealth is taxed in Australia by promising the budget will open the housing market to younger buyers.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Asher

media:group.media:description: Anthony Albanese (centre) has confirmed tonight's budget will include property tax reform.

item.title: Australian company Heytesbury buys mega fleet of 11 live export ships
item.description: Owned by the Holmes à Court family, Heytesbury runs 165,000 head of cattle in northern Australia and has just struck a deal with Vroon to buy its ships.
item.dc:creator: Matt Brann

media:group.media:description: The live export trade to Indonesia is dominated by G-Class vessels such as the Greyman Express.

item.title: Murdoch Uni admits accessibility needs fix after student's viral video
item.description: The former Murdoch University student's video highlights the difficulty wheelchair users have navigating the campus since stair lifts were removed in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Emma Wynne and Kate Leaver

media:group.media:description: Wheelchair user Mia Day said she created the video to show the impact of the removal of the stair lifts at Murdoch University.

item.title: Australian Jewish women experiencing mass antisemitism, research says
item.description: About 80 per cent of Jewish women in Australia have experienced antisemitism personally or against an immediate family member in the past two years, the royal commission has heard.
item.dc:creator: Phoebe Pin

media:group.media:description: Shirley Leader told the royal commission of the research from the National Council of Jewish Women of Australia.

item.title: Concerns 'wildlife will be lost' as trees felled for golf course revamp
item.description: Wildlife carers are concerned possums and birds will become casualties as trees are felled for the government's $45 million North Adelaide golf course redevelopment.

media:group.media:description: The government says the possum population across the site has also been carefully mapped.

item.title: Bungled Spirits ferry replacement project cost $717m more than forecast
item.description: The Spirit of Tasmania ferry replacement program has cost $717 million more than was forecast in the original business case, a parliamentary inquiry has heard.
item.dc:creator: Jano Gibson

media:group.media:description: The final piece of the new gantry has officially been put into place at the Berth 3 facility in Devonport.

item.title: Canberra teen charged with planning terrorist attack
item.description: The 17-year-old appeared in the ACT Children's Court today charged with one count of planning or preparing for a terrorist act and one count of transmitting violent extremist material.
item.dc:creator: Zaarkacha Marlan

media:group.media:description: The 17-year-old appeared in the ACT Childrens Court on Tuesday, charged with planning a terrorist attack.

item.title: Qld deputy premier uses parliamentary privilege to raise rumours of MPs' relationship
item.description: It comes after more than a week of scrutiny on ministerial lovers Tim Mander and Amanda Camm.
item.dc:creator: Alex Brewster

media:group.media:description: Queensland Deputy Premier Jarrod Bleijie used parliamentary privilege to make claims about a secret relationship between two Labor MPs.

item.title: Brisbane flips on promise to crack down on short-stay apps such as Airbnb
item.description: Brisbane City Council announces it will not go ahead with plans to regulate landlords' use of short-stay apps such as Airbnb and Stayz.
item.dc:creator: Kenji Sato

media:group.media:description: Adrian Schrinner will no longer seek to regulate landlords' use of short-stay apps in Brisbane.

item.title: Canvas developer Instructure says 'agreement' reached with hackers
item.description: The company at the centre of a cyber attack involving online learning platform Canvas, which is used right across Australia, says it has reached an "agreement" with the hackers responsible.
item.dc:creator: Monty Jacka

media:group.media:description: Canvas is a learning management software used by educational facilities across the world, and was recently at the centre of a data hack.

item.title: Former soldier jailed for trafficking cocaine while in Australian Army
item.description: The Supreme Court of Queensland hears Cullen, a former infantry soldier, was sourcing ounces of cocaine and selling it to fellow soldiers while in the Australian Army.
item.dc:creator: Chloe Chomicki

media:group.media:description: Jack Alex Cullen, 25, pleaded guilty to trafficking cocaine in the Supreme Court of Queensland.

item.title: Year 7 student trapped under train had bag snagged by mirror, school says
item.description: The St Aloysius College student remains in a serious condition in hospital after the incident yesterday afternoon.

media:group.media:description: Emergency services attend to the boy who was trapped under a train at North Melbourne Railway Station.

item.title: SA man forced to spend night in hospital ED due to lack of access cabs
item.description: A multiple sclerosis patient says he waited three hours for an access cab, only to be told in the middle of the night one would not be available, forcing him to spend the night at an Adelaide emergency room.
item.dc:creator: Josephine Lim

media:group.media:description: Jesse Frick spent a night at the Queen Elizabeth Hospital emergency room due to a lack of access taxis.

item.title: Four more ANU council members quit after Julie Bishop's shock resignation
item.description: The acting chancellor of the Australian National University confirms four more members of the university council have resigned, following last week's exit of former chancellor Julie Bishop.
item.dc:creator: Adam Shirley

media:group.media:description: The acting chancellor of the Australian National University in Canberra has confirmed four more members of the university council have resigned.

item.title: Tough 24 hours ahead for Keir Starmer as doubters call for resignation
item.description: UK cabinet will meet today, local time, amid growing calls for Keir Starmer to set out an exit plan.

media:group.media:description: Britain's Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer pauses as he delivers a speech, at the Coin Street Neighbourhood Centre in Waterloo, London, Monday May 11, 2026.

item.title: Controversial haka sees debate around anti-Indian racism come to a head in NZ
item.description: Indian communities across New Zealand say they are feeling "hounded" after a surge in racist incidents.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Costigan

media:group.media:description: Graffiti calling for violence against Indians out the front of Papatoetoe Central School in Auckland.

item.title: 'Leaks in the urinals': New exhibition explores media's role in political history
item.description: The new Press Gallery exhibition takes visitors inside the original working spaces at Old Parliament House where reporters worked between 1927 and 1988.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jennett

media:group.media:description: Malcolm Fraser leaves Parliament House after parliament was dissolved on November 11, 1975.

item.title: Carlton boss says Voss resignation proof club is in 'genuine transition'
item.description: Carlton CEO Graham Wright says the club will embark on a thorough process to determine the successor to Michael Voss.
item.dc:creator: Chris De Silva

media:group.media:description: Michael Voss walked out on Carlton on Tuesday morning after spending over four full seasons in charge.

item.title: New offences target 'kill cars' and recruitment of children by criminals
item.description: New offences and stronger punishments for the use of so-called kill cars and the recruitment of children by organised criminals have been introduced in the Parliament of NSW.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Corvo

media:group.media:description: The NSW government has introduced new legislation to tackle organised crime.
